story.iWant: a clear visual summary of my strengths and weaknesses
story.soThat: I understand what areas need focus and feel motivated to start training

tasks.task: Implement header section with title, subtitle, and completion time display
Build domain cards using shadcn/ui Card components (Number Sense, Spatial Awareness, Operations)
Add score visualization bars using Progress component with custom colors (coral/yellow/mint)
Implement color coding logic based on score thresholds (≤2.5 weak, 2.6-3.5 moderate, >3.5 strong)
Add domain icons and labels (🎯 Needs Focus, 🌱 Growing, ✨ Strength)
Create "Start Training" CTA button that navigates to /training with pre-loaded plan weights
Store results in Dexie before rendering summary
Add celebration confetti animation using Framer Motion on summary render
Implement accessibility features (screen reader announcements for each domain score)

acceptanceCriteria.given: the scoring algorithm is implemented (Story 2.5 complete)
acceptanceCriteria.when: I complete the final assessment question

then.criteria: Header displays "Your Number Sense Profile" title
Subtitle shows "Here's what we discovered about your strengths"
Completion time displays in format "Completed in X minutes, Y seconds"
Three domain cards render (Number Sense, Spatial Awareness, Operations)
Each card shows horizontal score bar (0-5 scale, filled to score level)
Weak domains (≤2.5) show coral (#E87461) background and "Needs Focus" label with 🎯 icon
Moderate domains (2.6-3.5) show yellow (#FFD56F) background and "Growing" label with 🌱 icon
Strong domains (>3.5) show mint (#A8E6CF) background and "Strength" label with ✨ icon
"Start Training" CTA button displays (primary coral color with arrow icon →)
Button navigates to /training route with plan weights pre-loaded
Results stored in Dexie before showing summary (prevents data loss on navigation)
Confetti burst animation plays on summary render using Framer Motion
Screen reader announces each domain score for accessibility

doc.section: Recharts 3.3.0 provides React chart components for Progress visualization (Epic: Progress). Radar chart for Confidence x Time visualization. React-based, responsive, accessible.
Framer Motion 12.23.24 provides animation library with reduced-motion support. Used for confetti animations, timers, toasts. Celebration animations for assessment completion.
Assessment maps to `features/assessment/` and `routes/AssessmentRoute.tsx`. Stores results in `assessments` table (Dexie). Multi-step wizard pattern with React Hook Form.
Primary coral (#E87461) for main CTAs. Secondary mint (#A8E6CF) for calm accents. Accent yellow (#FFD56F) for celebrations. Success green (#66BB6A) for correct answers and milestones.
Correct answer: Success green with checkmark, subtle celebration animation. Session complete: Accent yellow burst with confetti. Weak domains use coral, moderate use yellow, strong use mint.

Step 13 displays celebration "Assessment Complete! 🎉", summary cards (2-column grid) showing domain scores (e.g., "Number Sense: 3/5 stars visual"), key insight identifying primary focus area, "Start My First Training" CTA button (coral, 48px).
WCAG 2.1 AA compliance mandatory. Clear language, consistent patterns, generous spacing, calm aesthetic. Visual hierarchy with clear headings. Critical for dyscalculia users to reduce overwhelm.
ResultsSummary module displays visualization of strengths/weaknesses. Takes scoring output, produces visual display and training weights. Part of assessment completion flow.
DomainScores interface has number_sense, spatial, operations properties. Each contains: score (0-100), questionsCorrect, totalQuestions, averageResponseTime, isWeakness boolean (score < 60).
TrainingWeights has number_sense, spatial, operations weights (0.5 for strength to 2.0 for weakness), normalized to sum=1.0. Generated by ScoringService after assessment completion.
Results summary displays strengths (green checkmark) and weaknesses (orange warning) with domain labels. Clear visual distinction between performance levels.

artifact.purpose: Pure functions for scoring: calculateDomainScore, identifyWeaknesses, generateWeights
artifact.keyPattern: calculateDomainScore returns 0-5 scale. identifyWeaknesses categorizes by thresholds: ≤2.5 weak, 2.6-3.5 moderate, >3.5 strong. generateWeights assigns 2.0x (weak), 1.0x (moderate), 0.5x (strong), then normalizes to sum=1.0

artifact.purpose: Multi-step assessment wizard shell. Shows how assessment questions are orchestrated
artifact.keyPattern: Uses React Hook Form with Zod validation. Progress component shows completion percentage. Sheet component for full-screen modal. Navigation with Previous/Next buttons. onComplete callback receives all answers
artifact.usage: ResultsSummary will be called from onComplete handler after final question

artifact.purpose: shadcn/ui Progress bar component using Radix UI
artifact.keyPattern: Accepts value prop (0-100), renders horizontal bar with bg-primary/20 background and bg-primary fill. Smooth transition animation on value changes
artifact.usage: Use for horizontal score bars showing 0-5 scale per domain. Convert score to percentage: (score/5)*100

artifact.purpose: shadcn/ui Card components for structured content display
artifact.keyPattern: Card wrapper with rounded-xl, border, shadow. CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, CardFooter sub-components. Flexible layout system
artifact.usage: Use Card for each domain (Number Sense, Spatial, Operations). CardHeader for domain name, CardContent for score bar and label

artifact.purpose: shadcn/ui Button component with variant system
artifact.keyPattern: Variants: default (bg-primary), secondary (bg-secondary), destructive, outline, ghost, link. Size variants: default, sm, lg, icon. Focus-visible ring for accessibility
artifact.usage: "Start Training" CTA uses default variant (coral primary color). Ensure min-h-[44px] for touch targets

package.name: framer-motion
package.usage: Confetti animation on results summary render
package.example: Use motion components for celebration burst effect

package.name: lucide-react
package.usage: Icons for domain labels: Target icon for "Needs Focus", Sprout for "Growing", Sparkles for "Strength", ArrowRight for CTA button

package.name: dexie
package.usage: Store assessment results before rendering summary (AC12). Use db.assessments table
package.pattern: Import db from services/storage. Call db.assessments.add() with full Assessment record before showing ResultsSummary

package.name: react-router
package.usage: Navigate to /training route with training weights pre-loaded (AC11)
package.pattern: Use useNavigate hook to navigate. Pass state with training weights

constraints.constraint: Component location: src/features/assessment/components/ResultsSummary.tsx per task 1 and architecture feature-based organization
Must use shadcn/ui components: Card, Progress, Button. Follow Balanced Warmth color theme: coral #E87461 (weak), yellow #FFD56F (moderate), mint #A8E6CF (strong)
WCAG 2.1 AA compliance: 4.5:1 contrast ratios, ARIA labels for screen readers (AC14), keyboard navigation, 44px+ touch targets
Must store results in Dexie BEFORE rendering summary (AC12) to prevent data loss. Assessment completion triggers: score calculation → Dexie save → ResultsSummary display
Confetti animation must respect prefers-reduced-motion. Target 60fps for animations. Component render <100ms
100% test coverage required per architecture. Component tests in ResultsSummary.test.tsx (task 11). Test all acceptance criteria including screen reader announcements

properties.prop: Scores for all three domains from scoring service
Callback when Start Training button clicked (AC11)
Normalized weight 0-1 for number sense training prioritization
Normalized weight 0-1 for spatial training prioritization
Normalized weight 0-1 for operations training prioritization

standards.standard: 100% code coverage (statements, branches, functions, lines) per architecture requirement
Vitest + React Testing Library for component tests. Co-located test file: ResultsSummary.test.tsx
WCAG 2.1 AA compliance verified with axe accessibility testing
All acceptance criteria (AC1-AC14) must have corresponding test cases

locations.location: Already exists - scoring algorithm tests (reference for domain score logic)

ideas.idea: Test AC1-AC3: Verify header displays "Your Number Sense Profile", subtitle, and formatted completion time ("Completed in 3 minutes, 45 seconds")
Test AC4-AC6: Render three domain cards (Number Sense, Spatial Awareness, Operations), each with domain name and horizontal score bar (0-5 scale)
Test AC7-AC9: Color coding logic - weak (≤2.5) shows coral bg + "Needs Focus" 🎯, moderate (2.6-3.5) yellow bg + "Growing" 🌱, strong (>3.5) mint bg + "Strength" ✨
Test AC10-AC11: "Start Training" button renders with coral color, arrow icon, and calls onStartTraining callback when clicked
Test AC13: Confetti animation plays on component mount (mock framer-motion to verify animation triggered)
Test AC14: Screen reader accessibility - verify aria-live regions announce each domain score, aria-labels present on all interactive elements
Test score bar fill percentages: score 2.5 = 50% filled, score 5.0 = 100% filled
Test edge cases: all domains weak, all domains strong, mixed scores
Test reduced-motion preference: confetti animation disabled when prefers-reduced-motion: reduce
Test responsive layout: cards stack vertically on mobile, display side-by-side on larger screens
